# @ledewire/x402-client

> Runtime-agnostic x402 fetch wrapper for the Ledewire ledewire-wallet payment scheme.
> npm install @ledewire/x402-client
> Source: https://github.com/ledewire/ledewire-js-sdk
> API docs: https://ledewire.github.io/ledewire-js-sdk/api/

## Install

```bash
npm install @ledewire/x402-client
```

## Quick start

```ts
import { createLedewireFetch } from '@ledewire/x402-client'

const fetch = createLedewireFetch({
  key: process.env.LEDEWIRE_BUYER_KEY,
  secret: process.env.LEDEWIRE_BUYER_SECRET,
})

// Payment is handled automatically — this is all the agent author writes
const res = await fetch('https://blog.example.com/posts/great-article')
const article = await res.json()
```

## createLedewireFetch() configuration

```ts
const fetch = createLedewireFetch({
  key: string,       // Buyer API key (e.g. bktst_abc123)
  secret: string,    // 64-char hex secret, shown once at creation
  apiBase?: string,  // defaults to https://api.ledewire.com; overridden by server extension block
  fetch?: typeof globalThis.fetch,  // custom fetch for proxies or testing
})
```

The apiBase is self-configuring: the server's PAYMENT-REQUIRED response includes
extensions.ledewire-wallet.apiBase which takes precedence — same client works for
staging and production without configuration changes.

## x402 payment flow (transparent to caller)

1. First request → 402 + PAYMENT-REQUIRED header (base64 JSON)
2. Client authenticates with buyer API key → buyer JWT (cached, auto-refreshed)
3. Client reads extensions.ledewire-wallet.contentId and accepts[0].extra.nonce
4. Constructs PAYMENT-SIGNATURE: base64(JSON({ x402Version:2, resource:{url}, accepted, payload:{token,contentId} }))
5. Retries original request with PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header → 200 + content

## Error types

```ts
import {
  InsufficientFundsError,   // 422 — wallet balance too low
  NonceExpiredError,         // 402 — nonce expired, retry the fetch
  UnsupportedSchemeError,    // 402 — not a ledewire-wallet challenge
  MalformedPaymentRequiredError, // 402 — bad PAYMENT-REQUIRED structure
} from '@ledewire/x402-client'
import { AuthError, LedewireError } from '@ledewire/x402-client'
```

All errors extend LedewireError. Check .statusCode and .message.

## Transport adapters

The package exposes three layers — use the one that fits your HTTP client:

| Export | From | Use case |
|---|---|---|
| `createLedewireFetch({ key, secret })` | main export | Drop-in fetch replacement |
| `wrapFetchWithPayment(fetchFn, client)` | main export | Wrap a specific fetch instance |
| `wrapAxiosWithPayment(instance, client)` | `@ledewire/x402-client/axios` | Add interceptor to Axios |
| `client.buildPaymentSignature(header, url)` | `LedewirePaymentClient` | Any other HTTP client |

### Fetch (explicit)

```ts
import { LedewirePaymentClient, wrapFetchWithPayment } from '@ledewire/x402-client'

const client = new LedewirePaymentClient({ key, secret })
const fetch = wrapFetchWithPayment(globalThis.fetch, client)

const res = await fetch('https://blog.example.com/posts/article')
```

### Axios

```ts
import axios from 'axios'
import { LedewirePaymentClient } from '@ledewire/x402-client'
import { wrapAxiosWithPayment } from '@ledewire/x402-client/axios'

const client = new LedewirePaymentClient({ key, secret })
const api = wrapAxiosWithPayment(axios.create(), client)

const res = await api.get('https://blog.example.com/posts/article')
```

Requires `axios` peer dependency (`npm install axios`).

### Custom transport (ky, got, undici, etc.)

```ts
import { LedewirePaymentClient } from '@ledewire/x402-client'

const client = new LedewirePaymentClient({ key, secret })

// Inside your interceptor / hook:
const sig = await client.buildPaymentSignature(
  response.headers.get('payment-required'), // raw PAYMENT-REQUIRED header value
  request.url,                              // absolute URL of the resource
)
// Set header on retry: 'PAYMENT-SIGNATURE': sig
```

## LedewirePaymentClient

```ts
import { LedewirePaymentClient } from '@ledewire/x402-client'

const client = new LedewirePaymentClient({
  key: string,       // Buyer API key
  secret: string,    // 64-char hex secret
  apiBase?: string,  // defaults to https://api.ledewire.com
  fetch?: typeof globalThis.fetch,  // custom fetch for auth calls only
})

// Returns base64-encoded PAYMENT-SIGNATURE string
const sig = await client.buildPaymentSignature(paymentRequiredHeader, url)

// Internal auth manager — also exported separately
client.auth.apiBase  // mutable, updated from server extension block
```

## PaymentSigner interface

```ts
import type { PaymentSigner } from '@ledewire/x402-client'

// Both wrapFetchWithPayment and wrapAxiosWithPayment accept any PaymentSigner —
// useful for testing with a plain object mock:
const mock: PaymentSigner = {
  buildPaymentSignature: async (_header, _url) => btoa(JSON.stringify({ ... }))
}
```

## Wire format reference

PAYMENT-REQUIRED header (base64 JSON emitted by server on 402):
```json
{
  "x402Version": 2,
  "resource": { "url": "https://origin/post" },
  "accepts": [{
    "scheme": "ledewire-wallet", "network": "ledewire:v1",
    "amount": "100", "asset": "USD", "payTo": "store:<uuid>",
    "maxTimeoutSeconds": 60,
    "extra": { "nonce": "<single-use>", "expiresAt": 1234567890, "contentId": "<uuid>" }
  }],
  "extensions": {
    "ledewire-wallet": {
      "apiBase": "https://api.ledewire.com",
      "authEndpoint": "/v1/auth/login/buyer-api-key",
      "schemeVersion": "ledewire:v1",
      "contentId": "<uuid>"
    }
  }
}
```

PAYMENT-SIGNATURE header (base64 JSON sent by client on retry):
```json
{
  "x402Version": 2,
  "resource": { "url": "https://origin/post" },
  "accepted": { "scheme": "ledewire-wallet", "network": "ledewire:v1", "..." },
  "payload": { "token": "<buyer-JWT>", "contentId": "<uuid>" }
}
```

## Runtime compatibility

Web-standard APIs only (fetch, atob, btoa, Response, Headers, URL).
No Node.js-specific imports. Runs on Node 18+, Deno, Cloudflare Workers, Vercel Edge.

## Related packages

- @ledewire/node — full Ledewire Node.js client (buyer auth, wallet, merchant)
- @ledewire/browser — Ledewire browser SDK
